name: Qivalis Euro
launchPhaseDetail: 12-bank consortium (incl. ING, UniCredit, BNP Paribas, BBVA, CaixaBank); French Finance Minister Lescure publicly endorses project at Paris crypto conference (Apr 17) — sharp reversal from previous administration's hostility; EMI license application with Dutch Central Bank reportedly in final stages; advanced exchange/market-maker discussions (Bit2Me named); launching on Ethereum (primary), Polygon, Base with permissioned KYC layer

featuredContent.title: CaixaBank and Qivalis partner to launch a euro stablecoin
featuredContent.description: Spanish banking giant CaixaBank partners with Qivalis to develop a fully regulated euro stablecoin under MiCA, targeting crypto exchanges and DeFi protocols.

featuredContent.title: BNP Paribas joins European consortium to launch euro-backed stablecoin
featuredContent.description: 12 European banks — including ING, UniCredit, BNP Paribas, CaixaBank, and BBVA — unite under Qivalis for a MiCA-compliant euro stablecoin targeting H2 2026.

featuredContent.title: France's finance minister calls for more euro stablecoins, expresses Qivalis support
featuredContent.description: Roland Lescure reverses Paris's prior hostility to private stablecoins, specifically backing Qivalis and urging EU banks to accelerate tokenized deposits.

milestones.title: CaixaBank and Qivalis announce euro stablecoin initiative
BNP Paribas joins consortium, bringing total to 12 European banks
milestones.description: ING, UniCredit, BNP Paribas, CaixaBank, BBVA, Banca Sella, Danske Bank, DekaBank, DZ BANK, KBC, Raiffeisen Bank International, and SEB.

milestones.title: Advanced talks with crypto exchanges for day-one liquidity
milestones.description: Qivalis in discussions with exchanges, market makers, and liquidity providers for regulated platform listings.

milestones.title: CEO warns of 'digital dollarization' without euro stablecoin infrastructure
milestones.description: Jan-Oliver Sell highlights euro's 0.2% on-chain share; EMI license described as in final stages; positions Qivalis as euro infrastructure layer, not just a token.

milestones.title: French Finance Minister Lescure publicly endorses Qivalis and euro stablecoins
milestones.description: At a Paris crypto conference, Lescure called current euro stablecoin volumes 'not satisfactory' and urged EU banks to accelerate tokenized deposits — a sharp reversal from predecessor Bruno Le Maire's stance that private stablecoins had 'no place on European soil'.
